articles.title: Hackers Weaponize Balochistan Police Portal in Multi-Group Espionage Campaigns
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of sustained cyber espionage activity against several Pakistani law enforcement organizations undertaken by suspected China- and India-aligned threat actors between February 2024 and April 2026. "At Balochistan Police, the compromised assets included servers hosting web applications that manage police and citizen data, such as criminal and

articles.title: Six New U-Boot Flaws Could Let Malicious Images Crash Devices or Run Code at Boot
articles.summary: Researchers at firmware security firm Binarly have found six new flaws in U-Boot, the small program that starts up hardware as varied as home routers, smart cameras, and the management chips inside data-center servers. Four of the bugs can crash a device. The other two could let an attacker who slips a malicious image in front of the bootloader run their own code, before the device

articles.title: US Senator and close Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'
articles.summary: The influential politician had just returned from Kyiv, where he met Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday.

articles.title: NASA Scientists Take to Air and Space to Study Arctic Sea Ice
articles.summary: This month, engineers at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California are testing a spacecraft sensor that will help measure how quickly Arctic sea ice is disappearing. And while that instrument won’t launch for another year, scientists started preparing for its use during a recent field campaign in the Canadian wilderness. Researchers spent two weeks […]

articles.title: US launches fresh strikes as Iran closes Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The strikes follow an attack on a Cyprus -flagged vessel travelling through the critical waterway.

articles.title: Security Council LIVE: ‘Lost continuity of knowledge’ on Iran’s nuclear programme since US-Israel attacks, top UN official warns
articles.summary: Since the United States and Israel launched attacks on Iran in late February, there has been a “lost continuity of knowledge” on Tehran’s nuclear programme, Rosemary DiCarlo, the UN peace affairs chief, warned the Security Council on Friday as tensions mounted amid new strikes across the Middle East.

articles.title: Badly burned British couple rescued from ravine during Spain wildfires, reports say
articles.summary: The unnamed pair were found semi-conscious with 40% burns after being trapped by the blazes raging through Almeria province, according to local media

articles.title: China evacuates nearly two million people as powerful typhoon makes landfall
articles.summary: Schools, work and outdoor activities have been suspended in Zhejiang province and many transport services cancelled.

articles.title: Trump administration subpoenas New York Times journalists over Air Force One reporting
articles.summary: The reporters received the legal summons after they reported on alleged security issues with the president's new plane, which was gifted by Qatar.

articles.title: Polish PM pledges memorial to victims of WW2 'genocide by Ukrainian nationalists'
articles.summary: Warsaw and Kyiv have for decades been at loggerheads over the killings of many thousands of civilians during World War Two.

articles.title: ‘We face a new ordeal’: Gaza's search for the dead goes on
articles.summary: After thousands of buildings were destroyed in the Gaza Strip during the Israel-Hamas war, local teams and civil defense personnel continue the arduous and delicate mission to remove rubble and search for the remains of missing persons believed to still be buried under the debris of homes destroyed by Israeli airstrikes.

articles.title: New cholera outbreak alert for Sudan’s war-weary communities
articles.summary: In war-torn Sudan, a deadly new cholera outbreak has already claimed more than 100 lives, heightening serious concerns for vulnerable communities including in besieged El-Obeid, where daily drone attacks have continued to hamper aid access.

articles.title: Researcher Details WhatsApp-to-Host Attack Chain Using Three OpenClaw Flaws
articles.summary: Details have emerged about three now-patched security flaws in the OpenClaw personal artificial intelligence (AI) assistant that, if successfully exploited, could enable credential theft, privilege escalation, and arbitrary code execution on the host. A brief description of the high-severity vulnerabilities is as follows - GHSA-hjr6-g723-hmfm (CVSS score: 8.8) - An operating system

articles.title: New MODBEACON RAT Uses gRPC Streaming for Encrypted C2 Traffic
articles.summary: The China-linked cybercrime group known as Silver Fox has been attributed to a new Rust-based remote access trojan (RAR) called MODBEACON. Chinese cybersecurity company QiAnXin said that while the threat cluster may appear like a low-sophistication, high-activity operation that propagates malware via counterfeit installers using SEO poisoning techniques, it belies their true organizational

articles.title: Exposed Hacker Server Reveals WP-SHELLSTORM Backdooring Thousands of WordPress Sites
articles.summary: A cybercrime crew left one of its own servers wide open on the internet for three weeks, and it exposed the operation's inner workings: the hacking tools, the activity logs, and target lists naming more than 1.4 million websites. Far fewer were actually broken into, but the exposed files showed researchers how a mass site-hacking operation runs from the inside. The operation, now tracked as

articles.title: Iran war fuel shocks threaten Africa’s clean cooking push, IEA says
articles.summary: Disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz have hit supplies of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG), seen as a cleaner cooking alternative to charcoal and wood The post Iran war fuel shocks threaten Africa’s clean cooking push, IEA says appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: T. rex could become most expensive fossil ever - but it's a ​problem for scientists
articles.summary: A 67 million-year-old Tyrannosaurus rex will go on sale in New York with a pre-sale value of $30m.

articles.title: US Democrat Ro Khanna says he was detained by armed Israeli settlers
articles.summary: Khanna, 49, was visiting the occupied West Bank when he was held for 90 minutes, he says.

articles.title: Watch: Typhoon Bavi forecast to bring heavy rains and floods to Taiwan, Japan and China
articles.summary: See how the powerful storm is expected to move across east Asia on Saturday.

articles.title: South Sudan: UN acts ahead of drought to protect thousands
articles.summary: As drought risk intensifies in South Sudan’s Eastern Equatoria state, a new UN-backed plan aims to protect thousands of people before the worst impacts take hold.

articles.title: Peak heat in Europe just broke historic 1970s records
articles.summary: This year marks the hottest June recorded for Western Europe and the second warmest globally, according to the latest report from a climate tracking service released on Thursday.

articles.title: Critical Zimbra Flaw Could Let Crafted Emails Run Malicious Code in User Sessions
articles.summary: Zimbra is urging customers to apply updates to address a critical security vulnerability impacting the Classic Web Client that could result in arbitrary code execution. The vulnerability has been described as a case of stored cross-site scripting (XSS) that could allow specially crafted emails to execute malicious scripts in a user's session. It has yet to be assigned a CVE identifier. "The

articles.title: URGENT - Progress Tells ShareFile Customers to Shut Down Storage Zone Controllers Over Security Threat
articles.summary: Progress Software has told ShareFile customers to shut down the Windows servers running their Storage Zone Controllers, confirming to The Hacker News that it is responding to a "credible external security threat." The company has temporarily disabled access to the affected accounts, a step it says it took "out of an abundance of caution" while it works with internal and external security

articles.title: Laser Attack Resets Tangem Wallet Passwords on Cards That Can't Be Patched
articles.summary: Researchers at Ledger's Donjon security team have shown that a precisely timed laser pulse, aimed at the chip inside a Tangem crypto wallet card, can reset the card's password to anything the attacker picks. No old password. No backup card. Once it is reset, whoever did it controls the wallet and can move the coins out. This is not an emergency for most owners. The attack needs

articles.title: As blue economy gathers pace, communities must benefit from ocean boom, activists say
articles.summary: As governments turn to the ocean for jobs, growth and climate solutions, campaigners at the Our Ocean Conference in Mombasa warned coastal communities must not be left behind The post As blue economy gathers pace, communities must benefit from ocean boom, activists say appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: AI governance debate silent on risks to nature, campaigners warn
articles.summary: Global efforts to ensure AI does more good than harm must consider how it could fuel exploitation of biodiversity and ecosystems, conservationists say The post AI governance debate silent on risks to nature, campaigners warn appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: At least two killed in Toronto street festival shooting
articles.summary: Police say there was an exchange of gunfire between two individuals at the festival, and two firearms had been recovered from the scene.

articles.title: Muslim judge in India faces death threats after convicting 'cow vigilantes'
articles.summary: Tabassum Khan has been facing online abuse after convicting 14 Hindu men in a lynching case.

articles.title: South Africa World Cup midfielder Adams dies aged 25
articles.summary: South Africa and Mamelodi Sundowns midfielder Jayden Adams dies at the age of 25, just weeks after featuring for his country at the World Cup.

articles.title: East Asia braces for destructive typhoon as landslides kill 15 in Philippines
articles.summary: Heading for Taiwan and south-eastern China, the 1,000 km-wide Bavi is forecast to be one of the strongest storms in decades.

articles.title: World News in Brief: UN spotlights education aid solution, Sri Lanka prison violence, humanitarian aid to Venezuela
articles.summary: The UN Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) urged international lenders to expand debt-for-education swaps, warning in a Friday report that many developing countries are spending more on debt servicing than on schooling their children.

articles.title: Aid cuts leave at least one million women and girls without vital support
articles.summary: At least one million women and girls have lost access to critical humanitarian support since January 2025 as unprecedented aid cuts push women's organizations in crisis zones to the brink of collapse, the UN’s gender equality agency, UN Women, said on Friday.

articles.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Anil Menon Launch to Space Station
articles.summary: NASA astronaut Anil Menon will launch aboard the Roscosmos Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft to the International Space Station on Tuesday, July 14, accompanied by cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina, where they will join the Expedition 74 crew advancing scientific research. Menon, Dubrov, and Kikina will lift off at 10:47 a.m. EDT (7:47 p.m. Baikonur time) […]

articles.title: NASA Space Telescope Maps Magnetic Fields of ‘Lighthouse’ Pulsar
articles.summary: For the first time, scientists have used NASA’s IXPE (Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer) to directly measure the magnetic fields of PSR J1101−6101, a pulsar located within what is often referred to as the Lighthouse Nebula. The results provide new insight into the structure of some of the most extreme objects in the cosmos, as NASA […]

articles.title: More than 40 kidnapped children and teachers freed after Nigerian army operation
articles.summary: "I feel happy and elated… I feel joy," the head of the local teachers' union tells the BBC.

articles.title: Heatwave to stretch into second week despite cooler air for some this weekend
articles.summary: The sizzling heat continues into next week despite a drop in temperatures over the weekend.

articles.title: New dinosaur species as long as cricket pitch discovered in Thailand
articles.summary: The plant-eating dinosaur, named Uragasaurus kalasinensis, is thought to have lived about 150 million years ago.

articles.title: Boxy forms and odd lights: US declassifies more UFO files
articles.summary: The US government published a fourth instalment of unresolved cases, promising to release more files on a rolling basis.

articles.title: US-Iran war leaves shipping at near-standstill in Hormuz again
articles.summary: Renewed hostilities between the United States and Iran reportedly brought shipping to a near-standstill in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, leaving around 6,000 seafarers stranded aboard hundreds of vessels and Gulf countries on high alert for further attacks.
